header_text: Due to Hurricane Sandy, Subway service has been suspended. Stay informed thru your local News Outlets & www.mta.info we will update regularly
description_text: Due to Hurricane Sandy, Subway service has been suspended. Steps to bring back service: Assessing: We are continuing to assess the storm impact system-wide goal of restoring Subway service safely and reliably. Inspecting: We are evaluating the condition of electrical components, signals and other critical infrastructure. Mitigating efforts: As conditions improve, we are making adjustments to provide optimal levels of Subway service. Communicating with you: Monitor our website and news reports for the latest updates.

description_text: There are delays and service changes on the [B] and [Q] lines as a result of fallen tree limbs across the tracks south of Avenue M caused by today's high winds.We have personnel working to remove the tree limbs, but at this time there is no southbound service along the [B] and [Q] lines south of Prospect Park; There is limited southbound [B] and [Q] service between DeKalb Av and Prospect Park; Some Brighton Beach-bound [B] trains are operating over the [D] line from DeKalb Av to Coney Island-Stillwell Av and some Coney Island-Stillwell Av-bound [Q] trains are operating over the [N] line from Atlantic Av-Barclays Ctr.Northbound [B] and [Q] service is running local between Brighton Beach and Prospect Park.Expect delays in [B], [D], [N], and [Q] service.For service to/from bypassed stations, take northbound [B] or [Q] service.As an alternative, see a station agent to receive a courtesy pass for continuing service.Station-Specific Alternatives:. For service to/from Church Av, consider transferring between the nearby B35 bus and Coney Island-bound [F] service at Church Av and McDonald Av:  For service to/from all stations between Beverly Rd and Kings Hwy, consider taking the nearby B68 bus, operating on Coney Island Av.
